text: Late have I loved Thee, O Beauty ever ancient, ever new, late have I loved Thee!
author: St. Augustine

text: Thou hast made us for Thyself, O Lord, and our hearts are restless until they rest in Thee.
author: St. Augustine

text: The soul that walks in love neither tires others nor grows tired.
author: St. John of the Cross

text: Grant me, O Lord my God, a mind to know Thee, a heart to seek Thee, wisdom to find Thee, conduct pleasing to Thee, faithful perseverance in waiting for Thee, and a hope of finally embracing Thee.
author: St. Thomas Aquinas

text: Pray as though everything depended on God. Work as though everything depended on you.
author: St. Ignatius of Loyola

text: If you are what you should be, you will set the whole world ablaze!
author: St. Catherine of Siena

text: Do not fear what may happen tomorrow. The same loving Father who cares for you today will care for you tomorrow and every day.
author: St. Francis de Sales

text: Have patience with all things, but chiefly have patience with yourself.
author: St. Francis de Sales

text: Nothing great is ever achieved without much enduring.
author: St. Catherine of Siena

text: The Cross is the school of love.
author: St. Maximilian Kolbe

text: Let nothing disturb thee, nothing affright thee; all things are passing; God never changeth.
author: St. Teresa of Ávila

text: It is not the actual physical exertion that counts towards one's progress, nor the nature of the task, but the spirit of faith with which it is undertaken.
author: St. Francis Xavier

text: We must speak to God as a friend speaks to his friend, servant to his master; now asking some favour, now acknowledging our faults, and communicating to Him all that concerns us.
author: St. Ignatius of Loyola

text: If God sends you many sufferings, it is a sign that He has great plans for you and certainly wants to make you a saint.
author: St. Ignatius of Loyola

text: A man who governs his passions is master of the world. We must either command them or be enslaved by them.
author: St. Dominic

text: The love of God is not built on our feelings, but on our will to love God above all things, no matter how we feel.
author: St. Alphonsus Liguori
work: The Practice of the Love of Jesus Christ

text: There is no surer or more evident sign of predestination than devotion to Mary.
author: St. Alphonsus Liguori

text: Prayer is the inner bath of love into which the soul plunges itself.
author: St. John Vianney

text: When we go before the Blessed Sacrament, let us open our heart; our good God will open His.
author: St. John Vianney

text: In darkness and secure, by the secret ladder, disguised — ah, the blest adventure — in darkness and concealment, my house being now at rest.
author: St. John of the Cross

text: The surest way to determine whether one possesses the love of God is to see whether he loves his neighbour.
author: St. Teresa of Ávila

text: You pay God a compliment by asking great things of Him.
author: St. Teresa of Ávila

text: Our body has this defect that, the more it is provided care and comforts, the more needs and desires it finds.
author: St. Teresa of Ávila

text: The devil is afraid of us when we pray and make sacrifices. He is also afraid when we are humble and good.
author: St. Anthony of Padua

text: He who labours as he prays, lifts his heart to God with his hands.
author: St. Bernard of Clairvaux

text: What we need most in order to make progress is to be silent before this great God with our appetite and with our tongue, for the language He best hears is silent love.
author: St. John of the Cross

text: He who is not angry when there is just cause for anger is immoral. Why? Because anger looks to the good of justice. And if you can live amid injustice without anger, you are immoral as well as unjust.
author: St. Thomas Aquinas

text: The greatest glory we can give to God is to do His will in everything.
author: St. Alphonsus Liguori

text: Do you wish to rise? Begin by descending. You plan a tower that will pierce the clouds? Lay first the foundation of humility.
author: St. Augustine

text: The world promises you comfort, but you were not made for comfort. You were made for greatness.
author: Pope Benedict XVI

text: In my deepest wound I saw Your glory, and it dazzled me.
author: St. Augustine

text: The proud man counts his newspaper clippings — the humble man his blessings.
author: Ven. Fulton J. Sheen

text: If we wish to keep peace, we must not give way to idle talk.
author: Thomas à Kempis

text: We are not the sum of our weaknesses and failures; we are the sum of the Father's love for us.
author: St. John Paul II

text: Where there is no love, put love — and you will find love.
author: St. John of the Cross

text: When you look at the Crucifix, you understand how much Jesus loved you then. When you look at the Sacred Host, you understand how much Jesus loves you now.
author: Ven. Fulton J. Sheen

text: Lord, make me an instrument of Your peace. Where there is hatred, let me sow love.
author: St. Francis of Assisi

text: Our Lord does not come down from Heaven every day to lie in a golden ciborium. He comes to find another heaven which is infinitely dearer to Him — the heaven of our souls.
author: St. Thérèse of Lisieux

text: Miss no single opportunity of making some small sacrifice, here by a smiling look, there by a kindly word; always doing the smallest things right, and doing all for love.
author: St. Thérèse of Lisieux

text: It is Jesus that you seek when you dream of happiness; He is waiting for you when nothing else you find satisfies you.
author: St. John Paul II

text: The measure of love is to love without measure.
author: St. Bernard of Clairvaux

text: God does not demand that I be successful. God demands that I be faithful.
author: St. Teresa of Calcutta

text: We always find that those who walked closest to Christ were those who had to bear the greatest trials.
author: St. Teresa of Ávila

text: Without the burden of afflictions it is impossible to reach the height of grace. The gift of grace increases as the struggles increase.
author: St. Rose of Lima

text: You must accept your cross; if you carry it courageously it will carry you to heaven.
author: St. John Vianney

text: We become what we love and who we love shapes what we become.
author: St. Clare of Assisi

text: He is not served well who is served only out of obligation.
author: St. Ambrose

text: Consider that the sufferings of this present time are not worthy to be compared with the glory which shall be revealed in us.
author: St. Paul the Apostle

text: Love God, serve God: everything is in that.
author: St. Clare of Assisi

text: Do small things with great love.
author: St. Teresa of Calcutta

text: The rosary is the most excellent form of prayer and the most efficacious means of attaining eternal life.
author: Pope Leo XIII

text: Charity is certainly greater than any rule. Moreover, all rules must lead to charity.
author: St. Vincent de Paul

text: True devotion to Mary consists in three things: veneration, confidence, and love.
author: St. Alphonsus Liguori

text: Humility is the foundation of all the other virtues hence, in the soul in which this virtue does not exist there cannot be any other virtue except in mere appearance.
author: St. Augustine

text: The bread that you store up belongs to the hungry; the cloak that lies in your chest belongs to the naked; the gold you have hidden in the ground belongs to the poor.
author: St. Basil the Great

text: Not all of us can do great things. But we can do small things with great love.
author: St. Teresa of Calcutta

text: Prayer is nothing else than union with God.
author: St. John Vianney

text: To convert somebody, go and take them by the hand and guide them.
author: St. Thomas Aquinas

text: Spiritual reading is to the soul what food is to the body.
author: St. Athanasius

text: O happy penance, which merited for me so great a glory!
author: St. Mary Magdalene de Pazzi

text: Tribulation is a gift from God — one that He especially gives His special friends.
author: St. Thomas More

text: Mary is the star of the sea, for as sailors are guided to port by the ocean star, so Christians attain to glory through Mary's maternal intercession.
author: St. Thomas Aquinas

text: He who loses his life for My sake shall find it.
author: Our Lord Jesus Christ

text: You cannot be half a saint. You must be a whole saint or no saint at all.
author: St. Thérèse of Lisieux

text: A tree is known by its fruit; a man by his deeds. A good deed is never lost; he who sows courtesy reaps friendship.
author: St. Basil the Great

text: Give something, however small, to the one in need. For it is not small to one who has nothing. Neither is it small to God, if we have given what we could.
author: St. Gregory Nazianzen

text: Watch, O Lord, with those who wake, or watch, or weep tonight, and give Your angels and saints charge over those who sleep.
author: St. Augustine

text: In the evening of life, we will be judged on love alone.
author: St. John of the Cross

text: Every moment comes to us pregnant with a command from God, only to pass on and plunge into eternity, there to remain forever what we have made of it.
author: St. Francis de Sales

text: God thirsts to be thirsted for.
author: St. Gregory Nazianzen

text: Before all and above all, attention shall be paid to the care of the sick, so that they shall be served as if they were Christ Himself.
author: St. Benedict of Nursia

text: Lent is a time of going very deeply into ourselves... What is it that stands between us and God? Between us and our brothers and sisters? Between us and life, the life of the Spirit?
author: Catherine Doherty (Servant of God)

text: Christ with me, Christ before me, Christ behind me, Christ in me, Christ beneath me, Christ above me, Christ on my right, Christ on my left.
author: St. Patrick

text: I die the King's good servant, but God's first.
author: St. Thomas More

text: The only-begotten Son of God, wanting to make us sharers in His divinity, assumed our nature, so that He, made man, might make men gods.
author: St. Thomas Aquinas

text: We know certainly that our God calls us to a holy life. We know that He gives us every grace, every abundant grace; and though we are so weak of ourselves, this grace is able to carry us through every obstacle and difficulty.
author: St. Elizabeth Ann Seton

text: He who created you without you will not justify you without you.
author: St. Augustine

text: Lord, grant that I may always allow myself to be guided by You, always follow Your plans, and perfectly accomplish Your Holy Will. Grant that in all things I may look to You, and let my eye be fixed on You.
author: St. Gaspar del Bufalo

text: The more we empty ourselves, the more room we give God to fill us.
author: St. Teresa of Calcutta

text: Come now, little man, turn aside for a while from your daily employment, escape for a moment from the tumult of your thoughts. Put aside your weighty cares, let your burdensome distractions wait, free yourself awhile for God and rest awhile in Him.
author: St. Anselm of Canterbury

text: Joy is the most infallible sign of the presence of God.
author: Léon Bloy

text: Offer to God every suffering, and He will transform it into grace.
author: St. Padre Pio

text: The study of truth requires a considerable effort — which is why few are willing to undertake it out of love of knowledge.
author: St. Thomas Aquinas

text: Let us go forward in peace, our eyes upon heaven, the only one goal of our labours.
author: St. Thérèse of Lisieux

text: Be who God meant you to be and you will set the world on fire.
author: St. Catherine of Siena

text: There is nothing small in the service of God.
author: St. Francis de Sales

text: Go forth and set the world on fire.
author: St. Ignatius of Loyola

text: A single act of love makes the soul return to life.
author: St. Maximilian Kolbe

text: God loves each of us as if there were only one of us.
author: St. Augustine

text: Prayer ought to be short and pure, unless it be prolonged by the inspiration of divine grace.
author: St. Benedict of Nursia

text: The Christian does not think God will love us because we are good, but that God will make us good because He loves us.
author: C.S. Lewis (Anglican)

text: Our wretchedness is the throne of God's mercy.
author: St. Thérèse of Lisieux

text: It is better to say one Our Father fervently and devoutly than a thousand with no devotion and full of distraction.
author: St. Edmund

text: Fasting cleanses the soul, raises the mind, subjects one's flesh to the spirit, renders the heart contrite and humble.
author: St. Augustine

text: Do not grieve over the temptations you suffer. When the Lord intends to bestow a particular virtue on us, He often permits us first to be tempted by the opposite vice.
author: St. Philip Neri

text: The secret of happiness is to live moment by moment and to thank God for all that He, in His goodness, sends to us day after day.
author: St. Gianna Beretta Molla

text: It is a great misfortune for a soul that has been called to religious life to spend its time in earthly things.
author: St. John of the Cross
